articles.headline: World Cup final squads ranked: Of all 48 national teams, who can win this summer?
articles.description: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.

images.name: Pochettino: USMNT are moving in a good direction ahead of World Cup
images.caption: Mauricio Pochettino speaks after the USMNT's 3-2 win over Senegal ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Nigeria files World Cup complaint to FIFA over Congo DR
articles.description: Nigeria are hoping to revive their hopes of appearing at the 2026 World Cup with complaint to FIFA over the use of at least nine ineligible players by Congo DR.

images.name: DR Congo vs. Nigeria [1296x729]
images.caption: Nigeria were beaten on penalties by DR Congo in their 2026 World Cup playoff.
images.alt: Alex Iwobi of Nigeria in action against Aaron Wan-Bissaka of DR Congo during the FIFA World Cup 26 CAF qualifiers play-offs final match between Nigeria and DR Congo, in Rabat, Morocco, on 16 November 2025

articles.headline: Can Tunisia cause an upset in Group F?
articles.description: Ed Dove discusses Tunisia's chances of progression from Group F as they face tough opponents Japan, The Netherlands and a UEFA play off winner.

keyEvents.text: Goal! Central African Republic 1, Madagascar 0. Hugo Gambor (Central African Republic) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Gabriel Oualengbe.
Goal! Central African Republic 1, Madagascar 1. Rayan Raveloson (Madagascar) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Warren Caddy.
Goal! Central African Republic 1, Madagascar 2. Rayan Raveloson (Madagascar) header from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Warren Caddy.
Substitution, Central African Republic. Sadock Ndobé replaces Axel Urie.
Substitution, Madagascar. John Baggio replaces Marco Ilaimaharitra.
Warren Caddy (Madagascar) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Central African Republic. Bertillo Yangana-Ba-Komi replaces Jacob Youmbi.
Goal! Central African Republic 1, Madagascar 3. Arnaud Randrianantenaina (Madagascar) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Loïc Lapoussin.
Substitution, Central African Republic. Samuel Nlend replaces Donald Benamna.
Substitution, Central African Republic. Moustapha Djimet replaces Gabriel Oualengbe.
Substitution, Central African Republic. Chérubin Basse replaces Peter Guinari.
Substitution, Madagascar. El Hadary Raheriniaina replaces Arnaud Randrianantenaina.
Substitution, Madagascar. Tommy Iva replaces Loïc Lapoussin.
Bertillo Yangana-Ba-Komi (Central African Republic) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Madagascar. Njiva Rakotoharimalala replaces Clément Couturier.
Substitution, Madagascar. Lalaina Rafanomezantsoa replaces Warren Caddy.
Rado Rabemanantsoa (Madagascar) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Central African Republic 1, Madagascar 4. Lalaina Rafanomezantsoa (Madagascar) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Njiva Rakotoharimalala.
